description: Opt-in network reachability probe via `planner provider-probe` (Phase 3 P2). Both the subcommand AND env PLANNER_PROBE=1 must be active; missing either exits 2 with one-line stderr policy refusal. With both active, the openai_compatible adapter issues one GET {base_url.rstrip('/')}/models round-trip against a local sentinel endpoint and surfaces healthy=true|false on stdout JSON. Redaction covers Bearer / sk- / sk-ant- / gho_ across stdout + stderr + (--verbose) details.
assertions: PLANNER_PROBE=1 + subcommand invocation → exit 0 when endpoint returns 2xx
stdout is one-line JSON with provider / healthy / reason / latency_ms keys
raw secret tokens (Bearer / sk- / sk-ant- / gho_) NEVER appear in stdout or stderr, even when the upstream echoes them in body / headers
endpoint is GET {base_url.rstrip('/')}/models — NOT a /v1 prefix append; default OpenAI URL https://api.openai.com/v1 must yield https://api.openai.com/v1/models
no run directory, batch summary, or probe_history.jsonl is written to disk
no Python traceback reaches stderr in any failure mode (gate closed / NotImpl / unhealthy)
PLANNER_PROBE unset or != "1" → exit 2 with one-line stderr 'provider probe is opt-in only' policy refusal, zero network calls
NotImplementedError from a non-implementing adapter (deterministic / openai skeleton / anthropic skeleton) → exit 1 with structured stderr message
